_readme: Shiproom Dashboard Config. Fill in fields marked REQUIRED. Delete this _readme field when done.

dashboard.title: My Team Shiproom View
dashboard._title_example: Your team's dashboard title. Examples: 'Azure Core Shiproom View', 'Teams Backend Shiproom View'
dashboard.subtitle: Sprint Review Dashboard
dashboard._subtitle_example: Small text above the title. Usually keep as-is.
dashboard._team_name_example: Shown in the footer. Examples: 'Azure Core', 'Teams Backend', 'OneDrive Sync'
dashboard._source_label_example: Label for the Excel source link in footer. If empty, uses the Excel filename.

paths._excel_path_example: Full path to your Excel tracker.
paths._html_output_dir_example: Where to save the HTML. null = same folder as Excel.
paths._bug_data_fallback_path_example: Cached bug data JSON for offline use. null = always query ADO live.

excel._sprint_tab_pattern_example: How your sprint tabs are named. Variables: {month_short}=Mar, {year}=2026, {month_num}=03, {month_long}=March. Example result: 'Sprint Mar 2026'
excel._dependencies_tab_example: Tab name for external dependencies. Set to null to skip. Examples: 'Partner Dependencies', null
excel._em_tab_example: Tab name for EM-to-IC mapping. Set to null to skip. Examples: 'EM', 'Team Structure', null

ado._enabled_example: Set to true to enable ADO bug queries. Dashboard still works without it.
ado._organization_example: Your ADO org name (from https://dev.azure.com/{org}). Examples: 'onedrive', 'msazure', 'mycompany'
ado._project_example: Your ADO project name. Examples: 'ODSP Product Experiences', 'Azure DevOps'
ado._area_path_example: Your team's ADO area path for bug queries. Use double backslashes. Example: 'MyProject\\TeamA\\SubArea'
ado._iteration_path_template_example: Variables: {project}, {cy}=CY26, {half}=H1, {quarter}=Q1, {year}=2026, {month_num}=03, {month_short}=Mar. Adjust to match your ADO iteration naming.

icm._enabled_example: Set to true only if your team uses ICM. Most teams can leave this false.
icm._active_query_url_example: ICM saved query URL for active incidents.
icm._resolved_query_url_example: ICM saved query URL for resolved incidents.
icm._data_file_example: Filename for cached ICM data. Usually keep as-is.

em_layout._enabled_example: Set to true if you have an EM tab and want the Resource Utilization view. When false, Owners column shows Dev IC name instead of EM name.
em_layout._pairs_example: One entry per EM. Example for 2 EMs side-by-side: [{"em_name_row":2,"em_col":1,"directs_col":2}, {"em_name_row":2,"em_col":4,"directs_col":5}]

_priority_order_example: Priority labels and their sort order (lower = higher priority). Customize if your team uses different labels. Status order and rolled-out statuses are read directly from the Excel Config tab's StatusList column.
